title: Arachne fruticosa
taxonomy: Arachne fruticosa is a species of spiderwort, belonging to the family Commelinaceae. This classification places it within the order Commelinales and the class Liliopsida. As a member of the family Commelinaceae, Arachne fruticosa shares characteristics with other plants in this group. The Commelinaceae family comprises a diverse range of plants, often referred to as spiderworts or dayflowers. The classification of Arachne fruticosa reflects its unique characteristics and relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Arachne fruticosa, also known as Wild Cotton or Cotton Bush, is a perennial shrub native to Australia, characterized by its fluffy, cotton-like seeds and small, white flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in relieving symptoms of arthritis, wounds, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Arachne fruticosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Australian herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or infused in oil to make a topical cream, and is also used in tinctures and salves to support skin health and wound healing.
